activities_projects.title: Visualizing 'Dust of Snow'
activities_projects.objective: Understand imagery by drawing scenes from the poem.
steps: Read 'Dust of Snow' carefully
Draw the crow shaking snow from the hemlock tree
Show how the mood changes in your illustration
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will visualize imagery and mood.

activities_projects.title: Mood Chart
activities_projects.objective: Recognize the change in mood in 'Dust of Snow' and 'Fire and Ice'.
steps: Identify the mood at the start and end of each poem
Draw a simple two-column chart showing emotions
Write lines from the poem supporting each mood
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand mood changes in poems.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of moods and examples

activities_projects.title: Fire vs Ice Mini Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Explore the theme of desire and hate in 'Fire and Ice'.
steps: Read 'Fire and Ice'
Discuss what fire and ice represent in simple terms
Give one example from the poem for each
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will identify themes and symbols.

activities_projects.title: Poem Recitation
steps: Memorize 'Dust of Snow' or 'Fire and Ice'
Recite aloud in class with simple expression
Highlight any words that show mood change
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will practice reading with expression.

activities_projects.title: Symbol Identification
activities_projects.objective: Recognize simple symbols in the poems.
steps: List the symbols: crow, hemlock, fire, ice
Write what each symbol means in one sentence
Share findings with a partner

activities_projects.title: Draw Your Own Poem Scene
steps: Pick a natural incident from your life
Draw a small scene showing a mood change
Explain in one sentence how it relates to the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will connect personal experience with poetry.

activities_projects.title: Emotion Match Game
activities_projects.objective: Connect lines from poems with emotions.
materials: Flashcards with emotions: hope, regret, desire, hate
steps: Pick a line from the poem
Match it with the correct emotion card
Explain your choice in one sentence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will identify emotions in text.

activities_projects.title: Create a Poem Poster
activities_projects.objective: Show understanding of poem themes visually.
steps: Write the poem creatively on a poster
Add small drawings representing key images
Present the poster to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will integrate visual creativity with comprehension.

activities_projects.title: Simple Quote Illustration
activities_projects.objective: Interpret one important quote from the poems.
steps: Select a meaningful line from a poem
Draw a simple illustration representing it
Write one sentence explaining your illustration
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand and represent poetry visually.

activities_projects.title: Compare Two Poems – Simple Chart
steps: Make a two-column chart for 'Dust of Snow' and 'Fire and Ice'
Write one similarity and one difference for each poem
Discuss with a classmate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop introductory comparative skills.
